Newberry

Bringing ideas to life.
The single source of truthSupersedes v3.5
Start here

Introducing the brand

The definitive guide to my LinkedIn personal brand. Strategy first, visual system second. Everything I publish, and every asset built in Canva, traces back to this document.

The one rule that holds it together

Profile is credibility. Content is personality. The KPMG role anchors the profile furniture (headline, About, experience). The original-thinker brand lives in everything I publish. Profile copy shifts toward independent over time. The niche and the content never change. I post as Jamie, not as "KPMG's incubator guy".

Structured to map onto Canva's Brand Kit. Visual sections are tagged with the Canva slot they fill.

  1. 01Goals & Objectiveswhy this exists
  2. 02Positioningthe territory I claim
  3. 03Audiencewho I write to
  4. 04Personalitywe are / we are not

  12. 12Format Specs & Statusdimensions, what's next
Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.02
01

Goals & objectives

The headline goal

Be recognised as an original thinker who is honest, credible, and knows how to build successful product businesses. Distinctive enough to stand apart from the middle-of-the-feed corporate default, without trading credibility for novelty.

Sub-goals, in order

  • Stand out. A fresh brand that separates from boring corporate LinkedIn and earns a second look.
  • Grow reach and following. Consistent, distinctive output that compounds.
  • Attract the interesting. People and companies offering roles, collaborations, opportunities.
  • Build an audience I can later serve. A following that becomes the first market for a future venture.
02

Positioning

Positioning statement

"Bringing ideas to life."

The line others repeat when recommending me. Short, plain, confident. It claims a clear territory: the making, not just the thinking. Works as a tagline under my name, the spine of my About section, and the through-line every post traces back to. A point of view, not a job title.

The transition (profile only)

Today the profile leans on KPMG for credibility. As the body of work grows, profile copy shifts toward independent original thinker, eventually founder. A change of profile description, never of niche or content.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.03
03

Audience

Ambitious people who are curious about building. One clear disposition: they'd rather make something than just talk about it.

The core reader

A few years into a career, watching the gap between their ideas and what gets built, wanting to close it. Often a younger version of me. Curious, a little impatient with corporate theatre, technology-positive without being a specialist.

Also in the room

Product people and builders who want sharper thinking. Founders. Less-technical corporate people embracing technology rather than fearing it. The writing works because it leads with judgement, not jargon.

Before and after reading me

BeforeAfter
Overwhelmed by tech news, unsure what mattersHas a clear read on the one thing that matters
Suspects the consensus is wrong but can't say whyHas the argument, and confidence to hold it
Has ideas, no sense of how they become realSees the path from idea to built thing
Feels they should be doing something bolderSlightly braver. Wants to go make something
Write to one person

The voice speaks to the reader. Write as if to one specific curious person, not a crowd. "If you're early in your career and worried about this" beats "many professionals find that". Assume intelligence, never assume knowledge.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.04
04

Personality

The character anchor. Constant. It does not flex by topic, format or mood. Every piece of content should pass all four.

We areWe are not
Curious. asks the better question, follows the evidence.Credulous. not swept up in hype, not impressed by a press release.
Plain-spoken. clear, direct, says the real thing.Dumbed-down. never patronising, never sacrifices substance.
Builder-biased. grounded in making real things.Theoretical. not armchair commentary or frameworks for their own sake.
Honest. admits trade-offs, flags mistakes, credits the counter.Cynical. not sneering, not negative for engagement.

If the brand were a person

The friend who has actually shipped things, will tell you the truth about your idea over a coffee, and makes you want to go build something afterwards. Sharp but not superior. Sceptical but not jaded.

The test before publishing

Check the post against all four. A hot take with no building in it fails Builder-biased. A clever dunk fails Honest and Curious. If it only passes one or two, it's someone else's content, not mine.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.05
05

Tone of voiceCanva: Brand Voice

Voice is constant: the four traits made audible. Tone flexes by context. Default is warm, clear and direct, speaking to one reader.

Voice constants

  • Lead with the answer. Support second.
  • Speak to the reader: you, if you're.
  • Active voice, short-to-medium sentences.
  • Warm but not soft. Generous, never gushing.
  • UK English. £ GBP. UK framing.
  • Admit the trade-off. Credit the counter.

Punctuation

Rhythm comes from full stops, commas and colons. No em dashes. Where a thought needs a break, end the sentence and start again. Short sentences may be short. This keeps the warm voice from drifting into the breathless dash-heavy style that reads as AI.

Tone by context

ContextEnergyDepthPrinciple
News & InsightsMedium-highLow-medQuick, useful, my read on it
Thought PiecesHighMediumPersonality and edge. The sharper register
Best PracticeCalmHighConcrete and generous, no fluff
CommentsWarmLowHuman, short, no lecture

Prefer

  • use (not utilise)
  • build, ship, make (not execute)
  • idea, thing, product (not solution)
  • I think, here's why (not it's important to note)

Never

delve · leverage · unlock · supercharge · elevate · empower · harness · game-changer · revolutionary · transformative · cutting-edge · seamless · robust · synergy · holistic · realm · journey · thought leader · deep dive · ever-evolving · furthermore · moreover · "let's dive in" · "here's the thing" · em dashes · very / really / truly / simply

Content pillars & mix

~50% News & Insights (lead, trend-riding) · ~35% Thought Pieces (personality, direct and high-energy) · ~15% Product Best Practice (depth).

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.06
06

Colour paletteCanva: Colours

Primary colours carry the everyday work, secondary colours add accent, and a chart set covers data. Each colour does one job. Discipline here makes the feed cohere.

Primary Colours

Canvas
Light base. ~70% of surface. Always the text background.
#F8FAFC
Ink, Blue
Structure, links, headline accent words.
#0476D0
Dark Blue
Small-caps kickers, deep accents, high-contrast labels.
#024A86
Charcoal
All body text & headlines. The workhorse.
#1E293B
Slate
Secondary text on frosted panels.
#334155
Accent, Pink
CTA & emphasis only, including the emphasised chart point. Under 5% per slide.
#FF107A

Secondary Colours

Lilac
Mid Blue
Teal

Chart Colours

Chart Blue
Chart Lilac
Teal
Chart Pink
Chart Orange

Do

  • Keep canvas and charcoal carrying the weight.
  • Use pink for one moment per slide; a recurring pink through-line across the carousel is encouraged.
  • Keep secondary colours for accents, never for body text.

Don't

  • Set body text in pink or secondary colours.
  • Use more than one pink moment on a single slide.
  • Put text directly on the raw background.
Contrast rule

Every text/background pairing meets WCAG 4.5:1. Charcoal on the white frosted layer only (light mode). The fractal glass sits behind content, never under the words.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.07
07

TypographyCanva: Fonts

Two typefaces, both free in Canva. The system runs on extreme scale contrast: a big editorial serif headline against clean sans body. Body sits at 34 so it stays readable when the slide is viewed small in the LinkedIn feed.

Fraunces
Display: title, headlines, big numbers, quotes. Set in Slate.
SemiBold 600. Italic for emphasis words. Used for Hero, Title, Hook, Section header and Quote. Never body copy.
Inter
Body, UI, labels, small wordmarks
Weights 400–900. Ultra Bold 800 for the Kicker, Regular 400–500 for body.

Type styles (the Canva Brand Kit slots)

Sizes are Canva points on the 1080 × 1350 slide, anchored from the 24pt floor. The Pillow column is the identical size in render pixels. Colour rule: Slate #334155 for display and editorial text (headings, stats, quotes, chart and matrix labels, source lines); Charcoal #1E293B for body text in lists and grids beside icon tiles (S04, S07, S09); Ink #0476D0 for kickers, italic accent words, subtitles, axis labels and CTA directive lines. In doubt, Slate.

RoleFontStyleCanva ptPillow px
Hero numeral / Cover StatFraunces600200267
TitleFraunces600110147
CTA headlineFraunces60092123
Hook / QuoteFraunces600 (Quote italic)6891
Section header / SupportFraunces600 (Support italic)5269
SubtitleFraunces600 Italic4053
BodyInter400–5003445
Kicker / LabelInter8002432
Source / footnoteInter400 Italic2432
Footer wordmarkFraunces600—PNG: ~126px (statement slides) / ~95px (data slides)
DPI conversion rule

All type scale values in this spec are Canva points. When rendering in Python/Pillow multiply by 4/3. Example: 68pt Hook → 91px in Pillow. Hard floor: nothing below 24pt / 32px on any slide.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.08
07

Typography continued

Hook and Quote are one row at 68: the roman cut is the Hook and the heading at the top of every content slide; the italic cut is the pull quote. Section header / Support (52) works the same way one step down: roman for a mid-slide heading, italic for the support line (the sentence under a Big Stat, the cover support line). CTA headline (92) is the statement size on the CTA slides (S10 family), between Title and Hook. The Kicker is mandatory on every slide and carries the slide-type label (THE NUMBER, THE ODDS, FOLLOW), not a topic. The cover is the exception: its kicker is the pillar name (NEWS & INSIGHTS) set one step larger (28–30). On a template the cover kicker is the constant; inner kickers are written per post. See the shared layout rules in section 10. Hero numeral (200) is the top of the scale, used for a single hero figure as a graphic element. Any Fraunces headline at Title size or above uses the lower optical size of the typeface; the high optical size thins horizontal strokes until capitals with thin crossbars (H, E, F, A, T) stop reading at display size.

When to use Title vs CTA headline vs Hook/Quote vs Section/Support

Title (110): the cover slide's main statement, one per carousel. CTA headline (92): the statement on the closing CTA slides only. Hook / Quote (68): the scroll-stopping line and the heading at the top of every content slide; the italic cut is the pull quote. Section header / Support (52): the quieter display line; italic for support sentences, roman for mid-slide headings; accent rule capped at 40%. Subtitle (40) sits under a Title or Hook as a quieter expansion.

Accent word

Set key words in a Title or Hook in Fraunces italic coloured Ink #0476D0. Use as many words as the meaning needs, but keep accented words under 60% of the line. Section headers can use the same treatment, capped at 40%. Never colour the whole line, never use pink: pink is reserved for the call to action.

Setup gotcha

When setting these in the Brand Kit, some rows do not persist on first save (Title size can revert, Subtitle italic can drop). Reopen each row after saving and re-read the stored value. Re-apply and save again if it did not stick.

Do

  • Pair one big Fraunces hook with small Inter body.
  • Accent key words in Titles, Hooks and Section headers with Fraunces italic in Ink. Under 60% of Titles/Hooks, under 40% of Section headers.
  • Left-align text blocks. Strong hierarchy.
  • Anchor from the 24 floor and build up.

Don't

  • Set Fraunces small or as body copy.
  • Centre long text. Mix many Inter weights.
  • Let live text drop below 24.
  • Push any size above the 200 Hero ceiling, or inflate body for contrast.

In Canva: weights are named styles, not numbers. Semi Bold = 600, Ultra Bold = 800 (Canva's name for the 800 tier). Pick the named style from the dropdown; selecting a bold-family style lights the B indicator automatically, which is correct. Never press B on top. Italic is the I toggle (Subtitle, Quote). The Brand Kit row editor has no case or letter-spacing control, so set uppercase and tracking at the text box on each slide.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.09
08

Title mark (wordmark)Canva: Logos

There is no pictorial logo. The name is the brand. The title mark is the wordmark "Jamie Newberry." in Fraunces SemiBold with the pink full stop. The primary mark is the PNG wordmark-01-on-light.png; typeset versions are used only where the PNG cannot be placed.

Jamie Newberry.
Jamie
Newberry.
VariantFile / setWhere
Primary (01)wordmark-01-on-light.pngThe lead mark, everywhere on light: covers, headers, footer lockup
Stacked (02)wordmark-02-on-light / -on-dark.pngSquare and tight spaces: avatar contexts, banner corner
Stacked (03)wordmark-03-on-light / -on-dark.pngAlternate stacked arrangement, same uses
Headline reverseTypeset: Fraunces 600, white + pink stopReverse placements until the -on-dark PNGs are loaded
Footer lockupwordmark-01-on-light.pngFoot of every slide. Large (~126px) on statement slides, compact (~95px) on data slides. No page number.

Rules

  • All wordmarks set in Fraunces SemiBold with the pink full stop. The Inter small-wordmark variant is retired.
  • Footer lockup: the primary PNG centred horizontally, the pink dot baked in. Two sizes: the larger lockup (~126px height) on statement slides (covers, big stat, quote, the three CTAs); the compact lockup (~95px) on the working/data slides (process, chart, matrix, grid, funnel, list). No page numbers on any slide.
  • All marks centre-aligned when placed. Clear space = cap-height of the J.
  • Never stretch, recolour, or rebuild the marks as live text where a PNG exists.
Footer lockup is the attribution

The footer lockup carries the name on every carousel slide. The brand does not need a pictorial symbol to work: the wordmark and the slide system do the identifying.

Stacked PNGs missing

The four stacked files (02/03, light and dark) are named in the asset sheet but the PNGs are not in the project. Re-supply them to embed previews here and load them into the Canva Brand Kit. The stacked chip opposite is a typeset placeholder.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.010
08

Active assets

The active set: the primary wordmark, the stacked wordmarks (02/03, files pending), and the profile picture. Everything else in older asset files is retired: the Inter small wordmark, the avatar options, the old icon exports.

wordmark-01-on-light.png

Primary wordmark for light backgrounds, 1761 × 312 master (1680 × 226 trimmed), transparent. The pink dot is part of the PNG. Footer use: large lockup (~126px) on statement slides, compact (~95px) on data slides; centred.

profileradial.png

Circular crop with radial gradient. LinkedIn profile picture use only.

LinkedIn In-Bug

Used inside the pink pill on the Follow CTA slide (S10) only. Height 48px, vertically centred in the pill, 28px left padding inside the pill, with 60px reserved before the pill label text. Nowhere else on any slide.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.011
09

The slide system & squircle

Every carousel slide is a frosted-glass squircle panel over a fractal-glass background, baked into one background PNG so Canva only handles the text. The panel placement is identical on every slide, so text never needs realigning between slides.

The shape is the attached vector

The squircle is defined by the vector file squircle-36-shape.svg, the single source of truth. It is 800 × 1000 (4:5). Place it at the panel size below, uniform scaling only: never use preserveAspectRatio="none". The shape is mirror-symmetric left to right, but not top to bottom: the top corners are wider and flatter, the bottom corners tighter. Keep the vector the right way up; do not flip it vertically.

S01 Cover (v7) · V3 cool background, frosted squircle, Ink italic accent
1BackgroundFractal glass, full bleed.
2Squircle panelThe vector, 1032 × 1290. Content lives here.
3Footer lockupWordmark PNG, centred. No page number.

Panel dimensions (locked): 1080 × 1350 carousel

PropertyValue
Canvas / frame1080 × 1350px
Panel size1032 × 1290px
Panel aspect0.800 (exact 4:5)
Left / right margin24px each
Top / bottom margin30px each
PositionCentred. Box x [24, 1056], y [30, 1320]
Panel aspect is locked to 0.800

Side and top/bottom margins are coupled. Never change one without the other. The panel is 4:5 on a 4:5 canvas, so uniform scaling forces unequal margins (24 vs 30). Equal four-sided margins would distort the squircle. This is correct, not a mistake to fix.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.012
09

Slide system continued

Text-safe zone

Text-safe zoneValue
Safe text zonex 108 → 972 (864px wide)
Content topy ≈ 100 (kicker at 134/152)
Content floory 1180 (distribution target)
Bottom zone (below y 1050)left margin moves to x 140

Asymmetric by design: the top corners allow more width and a higher start, the bottom needs a deeper inset. Keep copy clear of the rounded corners and the top sheen. Confirmed against the locked v7 renders.

Top/bottom asymmetry

The shape is flatter and wider across the top, tighter at the bottom: the top flat run is about 31% of the panel width, the bottom about 19%. Practical effect: text can run wider near the top of the panel and must pull in slightly more near the bottom, where the corners turn sooner. The safe zone alongside reflects this.

Templates

Ten locked slide types run the carousel, specified in section 10 alongside the shared layout rules (kicker, footer lockup, swipe cue, vertical distribution). They replace the old three-template system. The safe text column is x 108 to 972 (width 864) inside the panel; any text below y 1050 starts at x 140 to clear the tighter bottom corners.

Glass treatment

Frosted panel

  • White fill at 78% opacity.
  • Top sheen: 25% to 0 over the top 35% of the panel.
  • 1px white edge ring at 90%.
  • Drop shadow: offset (0, 14px), colour #0F172A at 40%, blur 28.
Legibility

Every text/background pairing meets WCAG 4.5:1. Charcoal body on the near-white frost holds above 12:1 even where a bright part of the fractal background sits behind the panel, because the frost is opaque enough to control the effective background. The panel does not break legibility.

Margins are final for the 1080 × 1350 carousel only. The 1080 × 1080 square fallback and any other format need their own panel sizing at the same 0.800 aspect.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.013
10

Carousel templates

Ten locked slide types (v7, the locked set) plus two CTA variants. The background PNG (gradient + frosted panel) is baked; templates add only text and icon tiles. Every type obeys the shared rules below, then specifies only what is unique to it.

Shared layout rules

Kicker — every slide, no exception

  • Cover = pillar name, 28–30. Inner slides = slide-type label, 24.
  • x 108 (left edge of the safe zone).
  • y 134 on the Cover; y 152 on every other slide.
  • Inter 800, 24pt, ALL CAPS, letter-spacing +0.12em.
  • Ink Blue #0476D0.

Footer lockup

  • wordmark-01-on-light.png; large (~126px) on statement slides, compact (~95px) on data slides.
  • Centred: x = canvas centre − width/2; centre-line y 1248.
  • No page numbers on any slide.
  • No separate pink dot: it is baked into the PNG.

Swipe CTA element

  • glyph-swipe1 on a bg-pink tile, 144 × 144px.
  • Label "SWIPE": Inter 800, 24pt/32px, Accent Pink #FF107A, ALL CAPS, centred 12px below the tile.
  • Present on Cover (S01), Big Stat (S02) and Pull Quote (S03) only.
  • Bottom-right quadrant, vertically distributed with the main content.

Numbered-list numerals

  • Cycle: Ink #0476D0 → Pink #FF107A → Lilac #5A38FD → Dark Blue #024A86.
  • Never the lighter chart colours (#12B1FF etc.) for numerals.
Even vertical distribution

Content slides with 2–5 discrete elements distribute them evenly between the base of the hook title and y 1180. Formula: gap = (1180 − title_bottom) / (n_elements + 1). This is what keeps the set looking composed rather than stacked. In v7 it applies to every slide, including the CTA family: the closing block centres in the space left below the headline.

Bottom-zone left margin

Any text element below y 1050 uses left margin x 140, not the standard x 108. This keeps copy off the squircle's tighter bottom corner radius.

Sizes in the cards overleaf reference the type-scale rows in section 07; structural values (tile sizes, gaps) are render pixels from the v7 build. Gaps come from the even-distribution formula, not fixed offsets.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.014
10

The twelve templates

S01Cover
Wins the swipe. Slide 1 of every carousel; the single highest-leverage slide.
  • Pillar kicker (NEWS & INSIGHTS) at y 108, sized 28–30 (larger than inner kickers); Title with Fraunces-italic accent words in Ink.
  • Subtitle (Ink italic) in the left three-quarters; Swipe CTA in the right quarter; the row centred between title base and the y 1180 floor.
  • One hook (≤10 words), one focal point, left-aligned, ragged right.
S02Big Stat
Lands one number hard. Doubles as the slide-2 pattern interrupt on a shifted background variant.
  • Hero numeral (200pt row), Slate, upper-left.
  • Support line in the Section/Support row (52) directly beneath, accent word italic Ink.
  • Swipe CTA below the text block. One stat only; cite the source when the number is the claim.
S03Pull Quote
Lets one sharp line breathe.
  • Quote in the Hook/Quote row (68), Fraunces 600 italic, Slate, closing words accented Ink.
  • Quote marks in Fraunces, Mid Blue #82B1FF, set tight to the text.
  • Attribution Inter 700, Slate. Block vertically centred; Swipe CTA bottom-right. Quote ≤20 words.
S04Process Flow
Shows steps or a sequence.
  • Hook title under the kicker.
  • 3–5 steps: 148.5px icon tiles at the x 108 margin, vertical connectors colour-matched to the icon above, step text in Body (Charcoal) beside each tile.
  • Tiles cycle Blue → Pink → Lilac → Teal → Orange. Each step is one action; never more than 5.
Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.015
10

Templates continued

S05Bar Chart
Shows a comparison. The headline states the takeaway, never the chart name.
  • Hook title is the conclusion ("Even before AI."), not a label.
  • Rounded bars in chart colours; value labels and category labels both Inter Bold, baseline rule in Line grey.
  • Pink marks the emphasised data point, never a negative. Source line then an italic takeaway (Subtitle style) below. ≤5 series.
S062×2 Matrix
Positions options on two axes.
  • Hook title; 640px grid centred in the remaining space. Quadrant labels Fraunces, sublabels (spend/return) Inter.
  • Colour encodes quadrant quality, not the pink-warning rule: the bottom-left (low/low) is red (worst); the other weak quadrants are pink; the top-right (high/high) is blue (best); the two medium quadrants are purple.
  • Label boxes are deliberately set to different widths so all four labels wrap to exactly two lines, giving the quadrants equal visual weight. Axis labels in Kicker style (Inter 800 caps), y-axis rotated 90°; axis labels ≤4 words, quadrant labels ≤6.
S07Icon Grid
Delivers a parallel set of points at a glance.
  • Hook title; 2 × 3 grid of 120px icon tiles with a 60px gutter.
  • Item text Inter beside each tile, ≤5 words, vertically centred on the tile.
  • Tiles cycle the colour priority order. 4–6 items; if an item needs a sentence, it is a Numbered List slide.
S08Funnel
Shows drop-off through stages.
  • Hook title; three tapering segments, 160px deep with 18px gaps, centred.
  • Percentages in Fraunces centred in each segment: Charcoal on teal/orange fills, white on the rest. Stage names Inter 700 to the right.
  • Source in the 24pt italic row at the foot; the x 140 bottom-zone margin applies.
Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.016
10

Templates continued

S09Numbered List
Delivers a reference set ("The 5% do this").
  • Hook title; 4–7 items with hairline dividers, evenly distributed to y 1180.
  • Numerals Fraunces 600 at the CTA headline size, indented to x 153 (matching the S04 icon column), cycling Ink → Pink → Lilac → Dark Blue.
  • Item text in Body, Charcoal, ≤12 words per item, one to two lines.
S10CTA — Follow
Earns the follow. Designed with cover-level care; one ask only.
  • Kicker "FOLLOW"; statement in CTA headline (92) with an italic Ink accent.
  • Pink pill, 88px high, full radius: LinkedIn In-Bug at 48px (28px left padding, 60px before the label), label Inter 800, white.
  • Suffix line Inter 700, Slate, centred beneath. Pink is sanctioned here.
S10bCTA — Comment
Earns a comment. The default CTA: one specific, experience-based question.
  • Kicker "YOUR TURN"; the question in CTA headline (92), italic Ink close. ≤15 words, never yes/no.
  • 164px comment tile on a pink background, centred below the question.
  • Directive line in Fraunces italic, Ink, centred beneath the tile.
S10cCTA — Save
Earns a save or share for reference content.
  • Kicker "KEEP THIS"; the directive in CTA headline (92) with an italic Ink accent.
  • 164px share tile on a chart-blue background, centred.
  • Line in Fraunces italic, Ink, centred beneath. One ask; never stack follow + comment + save.

All twelve are re-rendered in v7: Ink Blue kickers, Hook/Quote at 68, CTA headlines at 92, even vertical distribution throughout. They replace the old three templates: Stat Cover, Infographic Cover and Standard Content are retired. Keep to 2–3 distinct layout types within a single carousel.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.017
11

BackgroundsCanva: Charts

Background: fractal glass (baseline)

The background behind the squircle is fractal glass: fine vertical fluted striations with a soft colour core bleeding through, full bleed at 1080 × 1350. It sits behind the frosted panel and never under live text. The frost flattens it to a near-white field where copy sits, so the striations read only at the panel edges. Four baked composites exist, each a clean 1080 × 1350 with the frosted panel flattened in.

V3 cool · default
V4 warm-pink
V2 pink
V1 warm · reserve

Variant-to-pillar mapping

VariantFilePillarUsage
V3 coolcompositeV3cool1080x1350.pngNews & InsightsDefault · ~50% of posts
V4 warm-pinkcompositeV4warmpink1080x1350.pngThought Pieces~35% of posts
V2 pinkcompositeV2pink1080x1350.pngBest Practice~15% of posts
V1 warmcompositeV1warm1080x1350.pngReserve / specialOccasional use

Selection priority when a post sits outside the pillar map: V3 cool → V2 pink → V1 warm → V4 warm-pink. V1's amber core sits outside the palette and is kept deliberately; do not recolour it.

Build: two stacked squircles

The frosted panel is built from two stacked squircle layers, not one: a base squircle plus a translucent squircle on top, which deepens the frost to the right opacity without going muddy. Both use the same vector at the same panel placement. The fractal background and the two panel layers are flattened into one PNG so Canva only adds text. A standalone transparent frosted-panel PNG also exists for one-off compositing.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.018
11

Charts & icons

Charts

  • One clean, internally consistent dataset per chart.
  • Chart colours: Blue primary, Lilac secondary, Teal and Orange supporting, Pink the emphasised data point.
  • Fills at ~80% opacity. Pink marks the one point you want read first, never a negative value.
  • No mixed-meaning axes. Don't fake the data.
  • Negatives or declines: charcoal or a desaturated tone, never a second bright colour.
  • Donut segments must sum to 100%.
  • No more than five series in one chart.
  • Labels never below 24.

Chart types: big stat, vertical bar, line, donut, horizontal ranking bars, stat-comparison pair. Infographics: timeline, 2×2 matrix, process flow, numbered list, comparison columns, pull-quote.

Icon tile system

Icons are composed from icons-sprite.svg: a line glyph (glyph-*, 51 glyphs, stroke inherits currentColor) layered over a background tile (bg-*, 9 rounded-square tiles with a gloss highlight). The full glyph list lives in icons-sprite-reference.md. Default tile size 96 × 96px; Swipe CTA tile 144 × 144px.

TokenSprite IDHexNote
C_BLUEbg-chart-blue#12B1FFPrimary
C_LILACbg-chart-lilac#9671F1Primary

Colour priority order

Sequential icon lists cycle Blue → Pink → Lilac → Teal → Orange.

Light-tile rule

Teal and orange backgrounds take the glyph in Charcoal #1E293B, not white. All other backgrounds use white glyphs.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.019
12

Format specs & status

LinkedIn dimensions

AssetSize (px)RatioNotes
Carousel (primary)1080 × 13504:5Portrait. Export PDF.
Square (fallback)1080 × 10801:1Safest cross-device.
Single image1200 × 15004:5Portrait gets most space.
Link / preview1200 × 6271.91:1Also the link-preview size.
Banner1584 × 3964:1Text clear of photo crop.
Featured1128 × 6351.78:1One per content pillar.

Locked

  • Positioning, audience, voice, four personality traits, content mix.
  • Fonts (Fraunces Semi Bold + Inter), primary + secondary + chart colours (chart pink = core pink #FF107A).
  • Title-mark identity (no pictorial logo, the name is the brand). Footer lockup is the wordmark PNG, centred, no page numbers.
  • Squircle slide system (shape defined by the vector), light mode only. Fractal glass baseline; four composite variants mapped to pillars. Panel locked at 1032 × 1290 (0.800 aspect) on the 1080 × 1350 carousel; side and top/bottom margins coupled.
  • The full 12-slide carousel, built in Canva (design DAHNNfv1MMw): Ink Blue slide-type kickers (pillar kicker on the cover), Hook/Quote 68, CTA headline 92, even vertical distribution, x108 margin, two footer sizes. Type scale locked in the Brand Kit as eight pre-tracked fonts (letter-spacing baked in). Colour rule locked: Slate display / Charcoal list body / Ink accents. Icon system: the original 51-glyph sprite plus 27 new glyphs (LinkedIn-action, content and editorial sets), over nine background tiles. Glyphs exported pre-coloured (white / charcoal / ink) as flat PNGs so no recolouring is needed in Canva.

Next

  • Publish the News & Insights carousel as a Canva Brand Template and post the first carousel. This is the posting/testing phase: prove the pipeline end to end before optimising.
  • Carousel copy is final: all 12 slides signed off (S10 reads “I share how to bring ideas to life”; S11 asks for the most common reason pilots fail). Kicker labels set without hyphens by choice: THE TRADE OFF, THE DROP OFF.
  • Build the other three pillar variants (Thought Pieces V4 warm-pink, Best Practice V2 pink, reserve V1 warm) by duplicating and swapping the background once the News & Insights template proves out.
  • Deferred: oversized quote-mark font (parked), stacked wordmark PNGs, profile banner/featured rebuild, performance feedback loop.
How to use this document

The single source of truth. If anything in Canva or on LinkedIn contradicts it, this wins. The visual sections map onto Canva's Brand Kit slots (tagged in each heading). Update this doc whenever the system evolves.

Jamie Newberry · Brand Guidelines v4.0 · Master20
